title: Homemade sausage roll
excerpt: I've always wanted to make my own sausage roll, but never quite got around to it. Now, after this, I'll never buy one again.
html: I've always wanted to make my own sausage roll, but never quite got around to it. Now, after this, I'll never buy one again.

recipe.title: Homemade sausage roll
recipe.author: Marinda Engelbrecht

items: 500 g pork sausage (not bangers, real boerewors-style sausage)

steps: Set the oven to 180 C and put baking paper on a baking tray
Let the pastry thaw – I take it out the night before.
Remove the sausages from their casings and put in a bowl.
Mix the onion, Worcestershire sauce, nutmeg, breadcrumbs, cumin and oregano through the meat well with your hands.
Sprinkle a little flour on your work surface and roll out one of the puff pastry rolls.
Make a "sausage" with half of the meat and place it about 7 cm from the long side on the pastry.
Roll the pastry up tightly into one long sausage.  Cut it into 4 and place on your baking tray with the seam facing down.
Repeat with the other pastry roll and the remaining meat.
Let it rest in the fridge for at least 1 hour.
Take a pair of scissors and snip 3 small notches on each raw sausage roll
Whisk the egg and milk together and brush over the sausage rolls.
Bake in the oven for 40 minutes or until the meat is cooked and the pastry is crisp and golden brown.

notes: You can also make the rolls smaller for bite-sized snacks to serve with a glass of wine.
I cooked 2 of mine in my air fryer and they turned out just as nicely. 180C for 10 minutes a side. Just keep an eye on them.
We love eating these with tomato sauce.
